what_it_does: This file is an overnight status ledger maintained by a lead session. It records what has been proven by running use, what is built but not yet end-to-end verified, what is only documented or planned, findings from cross-review, per-session lane updates, and irreversible/external decisions queued for Tim's morning review.
observations: It opens with trust metadata (author, date, verified status) and a high-bar recovery-gate rule: irreversible/external actions queue for Tim, never self-approve.
It uses a three-tier proof ledger: PROVEN-BY-USE, BUILT-NOT-YET-LEAD-VERIFIED, and DOCUMENTED-OR-PLANNED.
The channel layer is reported as 54/54 acceptance tests passed, with two lead-added restart-coverage tests.
The supervised wire is reported as write-path equals read-path confirmed by hand-tracing both halves.
Session-splicing / clone-fleet is reported as proven live with both compact and uuid-cut clone types round-tripped.
The noticeboard (cc_board) is reported as 26/26 acceptance tests green and proven by CLI and MCP tool use.
Heart H1.1 is reported as moving the cross-registry resolution and traversal seed, with 11/11 acceptance tests.
The recall-fork lane lists many completed beats (Phase-A, B-7 fix, beat-2 channel-scoped recall, beat-3 crossings graph, beat-4 distill scaffolding, recall↔board wire, clone fleet recall).
Axes are reported as locked by Tim arbitration: SCALE/HIERARCHY, TIME, FRAME/PERSPECTIVE, STATE/PHASE.
A FINDINGS section records an auto-committer hazard, a CHANNELS_DIR name collision now resolved, and a watcher death-window race left documented but not patched.
DECISIONS-FOR-MORNING queues eight items (numbered 1-4,6-8) for Tim, including clone-fleet launch, auto-committer scope, live cap, MCP reconnect, and cron arming.
It references many external artifacts by ID: commits, thread IDs, board items, memory references like [[reference-...]], and workflow IDs.
standouts: The proof-status taxonomy is the central organizing idea: real evidence moves items up, no green-paint.
Channel layer verification is reported as closed with 54/54 tests and hand-traced wire halves.
Session-splicing / clone-fleet is reported as the hallmark achievement, proven by live round-trip.
Noticeboard and Heart H1.1 are reported as built and proven, moving the cross-registry foundation.
The axes convergence (FRD + composition + recollection) is reported as Tim-arbitrated and locked.
The recall-fork's clone:// fleet-recall is reported as green end-to-end, making past eras semantically searchable.
Backfill authorization is reported as resolved via a standing rule that lead authorization carries Tim's weight.
conventions: Honest status reporting with explicit anti-green-paint language.
Trust tags and provenance metadata on entries.
Reversible/cross-reviewed intent standard; irreversible/external actions queued for Tim.
Per-session lane blocks with checkbox status updated by each session.
Commit hashes, test counts, and thread IDs used as evidence tokens.
Memory references in [[...]] format for cross-cutting topics.
Findings section separates resolved and open issues.
concerns: The file is extremely long and dense, making quick scanning difficult.
It relies heavily on external IDs and acronyms (FRD, P0, 5th wire, cc_board, MCP) without local definitions.
The DECISIONS-FOR-MORNING list skips item number 5, which may be a numbering error or an omitted entry.
Some status markers like [~] are ambiguous without a defined legend.
The watcher death-window race is intentionally left unpatched, which is documented but still a residual gap.
The concurrent auto-committer hazard is reported as a real operational risk that has not been acted on.
notes: An overnight proof ledger that separates real verification from committed-but-untested work and queues irreversible decisions for Tim.
questions: What is the exact architecture of the fabric, channel layer, and clone-fleet?
What do the thread IDs (e.g., g-1781490401, t-1781443715) point to?
What is the full schema and location of the proof ledger tiers?
What is the relationship between the company repository and the recollection repository?
What is the 5th wire in concrete interface terms?
What is the FRD (function-of-relative-definition.md) and how is it formalized?
What is the [instrument-surface] auto-committer loop and where is it configured?
What is the current state of the backfill and P0 indexing after the reported halt?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is an honest morning surface and proof ledger, and the file delivers exactly that with lanes, findings, and queued decisions.
apparent_themes: Radical honesty and no green-paint in status reporting.
Verification-first: only real runs/diffs/reviews move status up.
Reversible intent and high-bar recovery gates.
Coordination across multiple autonomous sessions/forks via a shared ledger.
Trust fabric and cross-review at the wires.
Common knowledge and unification across substrates.
Resource gating and explicit authorization for heavy or risky actions.
intention_signals: It is trying to create a single, trustworthy morning surface that prevents inflated progress reports, keeps risky or irreversible actions queued for human approval, and lets multiple parallel sessions coordinate without overwriting each other's truth claims.
novelty: The explicit anti-green-paint proof-status taxonomy, per-session lane blocks, and a decisions-for-morning queue embedded in a live overnight ledger is unusual; it treats status itself as a first-class substrate.
contribution: It contributes a reusable pattern for separating proven, built-but-unverified, and planned work; a per-session lane format for parallel coordination; a findings section for honest cross-review artifacts; and a queue for external/irreversible decisions.
summary_for_embedding: An overnight status ledger for a multi-session build, separating proven-by-use, built-but-not-verified, and planned work, with per-session lane updates, findings, and queued decisions for Tim.
intention_for_embedding: To give Tim an honest morning surface that shows what actually runs versus what is merely committed or planned, and to queue irreversible or external decisions for his direct approval.
